key_insight: In RL with verifiable rewards, orthonormal LoRA initialization is theoretically the closest low-rank approximation to full fine-tuning and can stabilize training better than prior structured inits.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical initialization rule for parameter-efficient RL fine-tuning of large language models, especially on math-reasoning tasks where training stability and performance matter.
method: Analyze LoRA under RLVR and compare initialization geometries to full fine-tuning.
Propose geometry-preserving orthonormal initialization, yielding RLPO and RLMO variants.
Explain why PiSSA and MiLoRA can degrade or destabilize training in RLVR.
evidence_signal: The paper reports experiments on mathematical reasoning benchmarks showing orthonormal initialization stabilizes RLVR training and outperforms standard LoRA, PiSSA, and MiLoRA.
watch_item: The gains are shown in RLVR math-reasoning settings, so the same initialization advantage may not transfer unchanged to other reward structures or tasks.
paper_title: Geometry-Preserving Orthonormal Initialization for Low-Rank Adaptation in RLVR
